stories.intent: I-031: V0.27.7 US-327 wired deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 (idempotent backfill of stranded server-side battery_health_log rows) + a --count-stranded pre-check. The plumbing fires (it found 5 stranded rows + triggered the backfill), but the backfill script itself fails in BOTH practical run-contexts: (Context 1, run from the Windows dev box via Git Bash) the Pi-side sqlite path `$HOME/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db` gets MSYS2 path-mangled to `C:/Program Files/Git/home/mcornelison/...` when interpolated into the remote `ssh ... sqlite3 -readonly <path>` command -> 'unable to open database file'. (Context 2, run directly on chi-srv-01) `loadServerCreds` (imported from apply_server_migrations.py) SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.10 to read DATABASE_URL -- but chi-srv-01 IS 10.27.27.10, so this ssh-to-self fails 'Host key verification failed'. The Step-4.6 wiring is correct; the host/path resolution under real conditions is the gap. Fix BOTH: (1) the remote-sqlite3 path is no longer MSYS-mangled from Git Bash (MSYS_NO_PATHCONV / single-quote so only the remote shell expands it / build the command so the leading `/` isn't treated as a Windows drive path); (2) when the server address resolves to localhost, loadServerCreds reads DATABASE_URL from the local .env instead of ssh-to-self (or accepts the self-loop host key gracefully).

stories.completionNotes: SHIPPED 2026-05-13. ROOT CAUSES (both confirmed via code-archaeology): Context 1 -- subprocess.run on Windows Git Bash passes argv to a MSYS2-built ssh.exe which rewrites `/path` items to `C:/Program Files/Git/path` BEFORE the binary launches; this happens regardless of any in-Python quoting because the conversion fires at the MSYS2 argv-translation layer, not in the shell. Context 2 -- loadServerCreds unconditionally builds `remote = f'{addrs.serverUser}@{addrs.serverHost}'` + SSHes there to read DATABASE_URL; when serverHost is 10.27.27.10 AND the script is running ON 10.27.27.10 this is a self-loop ssh that fails 'Host key verification failed'. FIXES (both keep doNotTouch honoured): (1) new `_buildSubprocessEnv()` helper that copies os.environ + seeds MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 + MSYS2_ARG_CONV_EXCL='*' (both ignored on non-MSYS platforms -- the fix is portable); `_defaultRunner` now passes `env=_buildSubprocessEnv()` to subprocess.run so every ssh call from this script + the sibling backfill script benefits. (2) new `_isLocalServer(serverHost, *, hostnameProbe, resolveProbe, localIpsProbe)` with 3 detection layers: loopback strings -> exact hostname match -> IP overlap; any probe failure folds to 'not local' so the legacy SSH path runs unchanged; `loadServerCreds` gains 2 keyword-only seams (localEnvPath + localServerCheck, both None-defaulted = legacy SSH); when the check returns True the helper reads DATABASE_URL from `<project_root>/.env` directly via the new `_readDatabaseUrlFromEnv()` parser. DSN parsing extracted to `_parseDatabaseUrlDsn()` so both paths share it. The backfill script needed no usage-site change because it aliases `loadServerCreds = _us209.loadServerCreds` + `_defaultRunner = _us209._defaultRunner` at module load -- both fixes propagate via the existing sibling-import seam. STOP CONDITIONS not triggered: layered local-detection rules + fail-safe-to-False resolve the localhost-ambiguity stop; MSYS_NO_PATHCONV is the documented MSYS2 escape and doesn't affect non-MSYS subprocess use. VERIFICATION: 25 new tests / 88 existing sibling tests pass / 1 PRE-EXISTING fast-suite failure ONLY (ralph.sh promise-tag-contract lint, outside scope). stories.title: sync_history started_at + completed_at both written in UTC -- B-079
stories.intent: B-079: every recent sync_history row has `completed_at - started_at == exactly 18000s` (5h00m00s = America/Chicago CDT offset) -- one of the two columns is written with a local-time clock, the other with UTC, within the same row. The sync-duration metric (`completed_at - started_at`) is therefore garbage (always ~5h). TD-027-class bug: a naive-timestamp writer that escaped the Sprint 14 sweep. The writer lives in src/server/api/sync.py (confirmed via grep; src/server/db/models.py:SyncHistory is the model). Fix: make both columns use UTC per the TD-027 / specs/standards.md convention (datetime.now(timezone.utc) on the Python side / canonical ISO-8601 UTC) -- whichever of started_at/completed_at is currently wrong gets flipped to match the correct one.

stories.title: orphan-cleanup.service I/O throttle + ordering -- TD-051
stories.intent: TD-051: V0.27.7 US-330 fixed the I-030 startup_log regression with a race-guard (detectBootReason retries `journalctl --list-boots` 3x when it times out under boot-time I/O contention). The root *cause* of that contention is the V0.27.6 US-322 orphan-cleanup.service: its timer has Persistent=true (so it fires at boot to catch up the missed nightly 03:00 run) and the service runs `cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py --execute` -- a DELETE against the Pi 5 SD-card SQLite DB -- concurrently with the launching eclipse-obd orchestrator. The race-guard absorbs the symptom; the durable complement is to (a) throttle orphan-cleanup.service's I/O via `IOSchedulingClass=idle` (best-effort -> only runs when nothing else needs the disk) and (b) order it `After=eclipse-obd.service` so the boot-time catch-up DELETE doesn't starve the orchestrator's early `journalctl` calls. Low urgency (the race-guard is sufficient); this is hardening + a cleaner boot.

stories.title: Investigate the 199 leaked realtime_data orphans -- Spool Story F
stories.intent: Spool 2026-05-12 Story F: after V0.27.6 US-322's orphan-cleanup landed, Pi-side `realtime_data` NULL-drive_id orphan count is 199 -- down from 61,293 pre-cleanup (99.7% reduction; US-322 working). The 199 leaked through. Hypotheses: (a) writes that arrived during the cleanup-timer's transaction window; (b) reconnect-noise polls between the timer's firings; (c) a guard the timer doesn't cover (e.g. PIDs that always poll before DriveDetector engages). This is 'make a working solution even better,' not 'fix broken' -- Spool's analytics already filter on drive_id IS NOT NULL, so the 199 rows are inert noise, not data poison. Fix: cluster the 199 by timestamp + PID, identify the dominant pattern, and either tighten the timer / add a writer-side guard (if it's between-timer noise) OR fix the cleanup's transactional isolation / add a final sweep (if it's a transaction race). The investigation + the writer-guard/cleanup fix are Drive-12-independent; the final 'post-Drive-12 orphan count stays <= 50' check is a validation refinement that needs Drive 12.

If the pattern instead points at the realtime_data WRITE path -- engine-off polls landing before DriveDetector engages -- add a writer-side guard in src/pi/obdii/database.py (or wherever realtime_data INSERTs happen) per the scope-fence amendment process)

grounding.value: Pre-flight code archaeology (in lieu of live `ssh chi-eclipse-01 sqlite3` cluster analysis -- same carry-forward pattern as US-330 / US-331 / US-335 in this sprint): hypothesis (a) 'transaction race during cleanup' is RULED OUT by clock semantics -- SQLite WAL serializes writers; rows written during the DELETE have timestamps > cutoff anyway (cutoff is now-24h, writer's utcIsoNow() is now); the race cannot cause LEAKAGE. Hypothesis (b)/(c) 'pre-engage / reconnect-noise polls' is SUPPORTED by reading the writer path (src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py:353 + src/pi/obdii/data/helpers.py:135) -- both INSERTs call getCurrentDriveId() at INSERT time and stamp NULL when no drive is active; drive_id is set at INSERT and never UPDATEd. 199 rows / 24h ~= 8/hour, consistent with engine-start engage-windows + brief reconnect noise.
grounding.source: src/pi/obdii/data/logger.py:353, src/pi/obdii/data/helpers.py:135, scripts/cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py runCleanup WHERE-clause semantics

grounding.value: Fix shape: 'tighten leak path' via a second cleanup pass with a tighter default cutoff (4h), NOT a writer-side guard. drive_id is set at INSERT-time and never updated, so any NULL row older than the maximum DriveDetector engage lag (~30s typically; 4h gives 480x diagnostic headroom) is permanently orphaned. Wired into main() as a default-ON second pass that the existing .service ExecStart picks up without any unit-file change (US-334's lane stays untouched). --no-recent-orphan-sweep preserves the legacy 24h-only behaviour for operators who need it.

doNotTouch honoured: Spool's analytics filter (drive_id IS NOT NULL) unchanged -- this is source-side noise reduction not filter-side; US-322's runCleanup function byte-untouched (still 24h main pass, 99.7%-reduction behaviour preserved); DriveDetector engage logic + realtime_data schema not touched; deploy/orphan-cleanup.service + .timer not touched (US-334's lane). The two-pass design composes cleanly with US-334's IOSchedulingClass=idle / After=eclipse-obd.service / Nice=10 changes.

(2) 'fix would require changing DriveDetector / realtime_data schema' -- NOT triggered; fix is entirely inside cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py. (3) 'US-334 conflict' -- NOT triggered; my fix avoids the .service file entirely.
